mnemonics.title: Function Definition Parts
mnemonics.content: REMEMBER: A FUNCTION NEEDS R-N-P-B: Return type, Name, Parameters (optional), Body (code block)

mnemonics.title: Call by Value vs Reference
mnemonics.content: CALL BY VALUE: COPY IN, ORIGINAL STAYS. CALL BY REFERENCE: ADDRESS IN, ORIGINAL CAN CHANGE.

mnemonics.title: Return Statement Rules
mnemonics.content: RETURN BRINGS BACK: Value returns, Execution stops, Control transfers, End of function reached if no return.

acronyms.expansion: Return type - Name - Parameter List (components of function)
acronyms.usage: Remember the three essential components when defining any function

acronyms.expansion: Call by Reference And Pointers (use pointers for call by reference)
acronyms.usage: To modify original values, use pointers with & in call and * in definition

visualTips.visualization: Draw arrows: MAIN calls FUNCTION -> arrow goes DOWN to function box -> function executes -> arrow goes UP back to main. Like a detour: you leave your work (main), do another task (function), then return to finish.
Imagine a recipe card (formal - template) vs the actual ingredients you use (actual - real values). They correspond positionally.
Think of a stack of plates. When main() calls function(), a new 'plate' (stack frame) is added with parameters. When function returns, that plate is removed.

analogies.analogy: A function is like a vending machine: you insert money (arguments), the machine processes (function body), and you get a product (return value). The machine does its job independently.

Calling a function is like making a phone call: you pause your current activity, your call connects to another 'location' (the function), you have a conversation (execution), then you hang up and return to what you were doing.

analogies.concept: Parameters as Letter and Return as Reply
analogies.analogy: Sending parameters to a function is like mailing a letter with information. The function processes and sends back a reply (return value). Call by value sends a photocopy; call by reference sends the original document.
